review_text: Great location, that's about it. Price $60 for what!! amenities dated, pool lets not go there, used our facilities in motorhome. NRMA Woodgate have ensuite sites for $55 ...just saying. Glad we only booked the 1 night.
PARK HAS IMPROVED BUT STILL ONE TWO MANY DRUNKS

We chose not to stay at this caravan park due to difficulties arranging a late check-in. Our arrival was delayed because of a flight landing at 7:30pm, and while approval was eventually given, it was made clear that management was unhappy about the request.
Having stayed at caravan parks all over Australia, including during peak periods, this is the first time I’ve encountered a situation where strict check-in cut-off times were assumed rather than clearly communicated at the time of booking.
We decided to cancel, as we didn’t feel comfortable bringing our family to stay somewhere where our arrival was clearly considered an inconvenience.
Prospective guests may wish to confirm arrival expectations very clearly in advance.
Old park but I love it. Dog friendly is the plus
Don‘t expect to reach someone helpful on the phone when trying to make reservation. Not helpful, rude and ended call in the middle of a sentence. Good luck with this site.
It's quiet and peaceful.
A no frills Caravan park but everything you need. Huge camp-sites, friendly staff. A relaxing and quiet place to call home away from home. Great rates. We would be happy to return.

Really enjoyed our stay here. Showers were amazing- good pressure, heat. Location is good for the Fraser tour and harbour view, right by the boat club which was lovely but a bit far from the actual town. Fellow campers and staff were lovely. Definitely a few who are there long term/ living but that didn’t bother us as everyone was friendly and helpful.

Old and tired facilities. Grounds not well looked after and a lot of permanent residents
Toilet and showers need a refurbishment
If you have a big rig parking can be difficult and stressful

I called this morning for a reservation. The person on the phone was rude and told me they were closed during office hours. Not trying to even find a solution.
Would recommend
It needs lots of work.  Everything is falling apart.  Safety tape around the pool. Tapes dripping.  The only one thing is the toilets are cleaned every day and that makes a big difference.

Having read some reviews after we had booked our site for Christmas and was surprised that most of the negative reviews were months and even years old.
The service was great the gentleman that took my booking end of Nov as they didn't take booking for Christmas until Dec, he also allowed friends to book in for 3 days when the office was closed which they normally wouldn't.
Overall the camp was basic but live able toilets and showers were clean but yes could do with an update.
Noise from the fish market wasn't as bad as some reviews stated.
We enjoyed our 7 days there and the permanent residents very friendly..

Caravan park, is basic, but we have been staying here for 6 months, and needed to put our rv in repair for 1 week, the owners were kind and understanding allowing us to stay in a cabin for short time, thankyou and Godbless, we had no other accomodation. :)
Park a little run down, but cheaper than the others in town, so close to the boat club , 1 minute walk.

Very disappointed. I rang this campervan park to book a campsite . Male staff member was very rude over the phone . Hung up on me while I was speaking to him . ||The gentleman said there was a site available but he couldn’t book it for me . When I looked for reassurance that I would be able to book the next morning he replied “ Office is closed . Office is closed”. He then hung up on me. ||Very bad customer service and rude member of staff . Do not recommend contacting this campervan park.

Perfect location if you’re planning to visit K’gari Island. There’s the port next door and also options to book trips to Fraser Island at the reception.
Convenient camp site with proper utilities and amenities, clean and neat.
Easy to book, took us just a short phone call and all was set. Price wise the cheapest campsite with full utilities so far in our 2 weeks on the east coast.
Outdated park close to boat ramp

I don't know why people complain so much, the campsite is fine, it's the cheapest. The toilets are clean, they will always be better than the public ones and for that price... What more do you want? Good location if you have an excursion to Fraser Island, the web site is quite old don't allow you to pay and book, will be easier for the person, they will improve nothing to add.  Cheap and very normal

Der Campingplatz ist für Ausflüge nach Fraser Island günstig in der Nähe des Hafens gelegen, da wir vom Tourist-Service abgeholt wurden.

Alle Serviceeinrichtungen sind sehr alt, abgenutzt und schlecht gewartet. Eine Instandsetzung ist erforderlich. Die Sauberkeit ist akzeptabel. Für zwei Nächte okay.

Bis etwa Mitternacht ist es durch den umliegenden Verkehr sehr laut.

Wie zahlten je Nacht mit einem Campervan und Strom für zwei Personen 45 AUD.

Die Rezeption ist täglich nur von 8:00 - 12:00 Uhr besetzt. Anmelden kann man sich per E-Mail. Dann wird am Eingang eine Benachrichtigung mit dem Plan und zugewiesenem Platz hinterlegt.

This caravan park is very run down. The noise from the adjacent fish co-op can get extremely noisy day and night at times. Amenities are very poor, cleaning of amenities needs severe attention, watch cleaners apart from one use same cloth on toilets, benches and basins but no product, mop was disgusting. Camp kitchen does NOT get attended to and is broken, filthy and is an embarrassment and I feel for backpackers, tents, residents who use this as they have no other option. I spoke to office about cleaning to lady at desk who informed me it had nothing to do with her, to find out later she was one of the caretakers, if not caretakers role who's is it. They are more concerned about boat club. I feel sorry for residing residents and boat club should be ashamed that they treat residents as second class humans
The place is a but run down, still clean and tidy though and has all the facilities you need. The location is perfect, right next to the boat ramp.

Harbour View CP was a great spot to base ourselves for a week. Yes the park is run down and things aren’t shiny and new but… the sites were easy to get in to, plenty of room, literally a multitude of eateries at your door with the marina adjacent plus all your whale watch tour experiences as well.
It’s the cheapest park in town and we found it sufficient to all our needs.
If you are looking for fancy resort style accommodation this place ain’t for you!
But if you need a base for your van while you explore the area or do some trips, and somewhere to sleep safe at night I can recommend.

The owner or the Caravan Park Barry is the kindest and most accomodating man you'll ever meet! He has so much local knowledge of the area and is well respected and loved in his community and was so incredibly helpful in connecting me with tour operators and supporting me with all the information I required during my stay in Hervey Bay. His assistance was so greatly appreciated and made me feel very welcome in the Bay from the outset. The caravan park is in a great location and nice and quiet and very well kept. Couldn't reccomend this place highly enough. 10/10!
Arrived to check in at 2pm only to find the office was closed for the day, great customer service!
Quaint, clean and friendly  little caravan park close to everything.

Nice park Friendly staff
Had issues with power on first site but moved to the next site over and a different power pole all good
We were supposed to be on site 11 but don't know why we were moved as 11 was perfect for our small business and trailer plus nearer to our friends
Sadly to far out of town for us and riding a bike into town is not for the faint hearted

We stayed in a cabin at Harbour View caravan park. Don’t write this little park off. Go back and check out the hard work of the new owners. Our experience was terrific.  Beautiful bed , a comfy couch to watch tv and clean bathroom. |Yes a little dated, but give me any caravan park of the same vintage that isn’t.  Mozzies are always a problem and flies are just a pest too. Weather was good. We enjoyed our stay and would choose to stay again. |There are multiple coffee shops and carts in the township. Mobil service station has been revamped and looking so much better with hot food .

Large spread out sites, plenty of room for our boats.  Close to boat ramp (only 200 metres away).  Has a boat wash & fish cleaning station too.
Getting plenty of crabs 🦀 & King George Whiting as per photos.  Has swimming pool & happy hour is at 5pm daily.

Great views, big sites, clean amenities and pool area.
So quiet
Clean, well kept, accommodating management and great location.|Stone's throw from water and town.|Really enjoyed our stay !

Great quiet location with beautiful view over the harbour.  The cabins are well equipped and comfortable. Managers were very friendly. |Only a few minutes drive into town.   The way the park is set out parking was never a problem getting in or out.

Wow isn't this a hidden gem a little way out of town but worth it one of the best since leaving Perth
Massive sites well sheltered and beautifully kept grounds swimming pool and great children's play ground and accommodating management all for $35 a night for powered site

Just few minutes from Cowell. We have the cabin number 7.  Good position, clean and with normal kitchenware. Patio with view on green where kangaroos are in the morning. To complete the remodeling need only the change of the plastic on the floor.

This is one of the best, the managers are awesome. We came here a group of 15 people in 5 vehicles, used their powered sites and had amazing time. Please visit Cowell and stay here to support such a beautiful community.

We stayed for one night in a cabin prior to the Cleve Running Festival. Excellent uninterrupted views of the harbour and only a very short drive into Cowell. The bed was clean and comfortable. The cabins are dated, and we found that ours hadn't been cleaned very well - food stuck to the floor, hair on the chairs, unclean dishes, dust etc. There also was no hand towel provided. When checking in, the lady was polite, although we weren't offered any information about the park or Cowell. Having said all of that, I would stay here as a stopover again in the future.
Management very obliging. Ablutions clean and tidy. One block currently has no hot water (18th Sep).
This place is quiet and peaceful. I love it

This is a great park with views over the bay. The sites are super spacious and it is a large park. It had everything we needed, clean amenities, laundry and good access. I would highly recommend this park. I have been to 27 caravan parks in the last few months and this is one of the best. 5 stars.

Had a drive though powered caravan site for 2 nights in April 2024.  Older park. Quiet location just out of town. Bush setting, dry and dusty sites. We seemed to be the only people in the park that didn't have a fishing boat ! Pleasant reception.
Loverly site - dirt sites but that suites us well and it is dry SA, owners are loverly and overall a top stop.

I stayed with my partner for a couple of weeks,
All round a pleasant friendly experience.

The owners/managers Judy and dale work above and beyond to maintain beautiful greenery, ***Plenty of shade, Massive sites, Good wind covour*** many chirping birds and you may even spot a lizard.
old in need of new but always clean shower and toilet facilities, there are worse in surrounding parks that I have seen.
Good washing machines.

As the name says there is a beautiful view of the nearby cowell Bay.

This park has more than any park in the area!

With a well serviced clean swimming pool for those hot days.

Beachside access.

A next to brand new kitchen, including t.v and lounge, the kitchen you will find many mature age friendly welcoming people entertaining at happy hour most afternoons.

Beside the close nit mature age couples that seem to of been coming for decades, there are always families young couples staying letting their kids run free in the park playground, a trusted family orientated environment.
The owners are fussy with who enters, being always booked out.

There is a boat ramp at the parks door step, fish cleaning facilities provided.
Oysters, squid and blue swimmers in abundance.

My partner and I rarely have to fish with all the beautiful people at this park who always bringing us over there catch.

In town they have a large grocery store, bakery, fish and chip shop and pubs are all fantastic, I'm glad we stopped.

Wonderful stay here with bay views.  Fully informed by management on which way the sun will be and when strong winds are expected etc. and very accomodating.  Spacious sites. Lovely  pool & amenities.  Good value.  Short drive into town but nice & quiet.  Boat ramp very close too & dog friendly.  Go into town on a Wednesday night for spit roast pork meal $18. (The pub across from the foreshore that is)

My second visit to this park, I stayed in one of the ensuite cabins. It was very clean and comfortable. The owners were extremely welcoming and made my stay even better. Thank you and I will be back again.

Far from being our best camping from Perth to Adelaide. Checkin closes at 6 pm and even if one calls them in advance they wouldn't make even a 5 min exception, even though the owners literally live inside the campground. Mosquitoes at night, but maybe because of the weather.  Swimming pool opens at 10 am (after check out) and closes at 6pm, which conveniently prevents any use if you come for one night. On the plus side it wasn't too expensive, the campground is quiet and facilities are clean. Can't judge the rooms as had a campervan. Given the other reviews, maybe they have returning guests and don't necessarily expect tourists. All in all I'd pass (and pass Cowell too...) unless you don't have a choice.
Clean amenities, close to town, quiet, great pool and beautiful setting. Generous size for powered site. Owners are fantastic. Highly recommend anyone to stay here.
What an awesome park great bush setting staff are fantastic and amenities spotless, great views of the harbour would highly recommend
Stayed at a powered sites overlooking the harbour.  Such a beautiful relaxing place,  sites not too close and a lovely rural setting.  Couldn't fault it.

Loved our few days at this quiet spacious bush camp.  No problem to pick a preferred spot  up front for a water view or up the back for privacy and wind shelter.  Super clean amenities, hot showers, peaceful bush surroundings, couple minutes from town, sure you can stay hooked up if needed. $35 a night power and water.
